what_is_open: In the weak-overlap strict-gap branch gamma>0 with g(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma), determine the sharp welfare-regret exponent attainable by genuinely feasible procedures, including the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm and possibly other feasible policy learners.
obstruction: The available lower bound thm:minimax-lower is calibrated only to the information needed to distinguish the policy label on the active block and yields the exponent r_star. It does not force the additional weak-arm outcome-regression learning cost that appears in the feasible upper bound through the additive clipped nuisance drift r_mu,n r_e,n/q + r_mu,n q^{1/(2 gamma)}. Conversely, oeq:feasible-upper proves only an achievability result for the declared clipped-AIPW ERM with exponent g in the strict-gap branch; it does not exclude another feasible estimator from attaining r_star.
attempted: Combining the two-point/fuzzy lower construction with the feasible clipped-AIPW upper closes the equal-exponent branch. In the strict-gap branch this combination gives only the honest bracket c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-g(a,c,gamma)}(log n)^p. The existing Le Cam witness cannot by itself prove a slower lower bound, because its Hellinger/KL calibration is already balanced at h_n^{2+alpha+beta_ag} and does not encode first-stage nuisance estimation difficulty.
partial_result: If g(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), then r_feas=r_star and thm:rate-characterization gives c n^{-r_star} <= M_n <= C n^{-r_star}(log n)^p, resolving the minimax polynomial exponent up to logarithms. If g(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma), the strongest proved statement is the bracket c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-g(a,c,gamma)}(log n)^p; whether this bracket is tight remains open.
